codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking watch-startup determinism defect: create-event classification now suppresses historical create emissions for rows that existed before subscription start while preserving runtime ack and new-message lifecycle emissions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-worker procedure requires manual CLI sanity checks for touched command paths, but this fix run reported only automated checks while still setting skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 6 requires manual CLI sanity checks and step 8 requires accurate followedProcedure reporting. Transcript skeleton for session a67f5ebc-1ca2-4fcb-a3d9-08a6eff803e9 and its handoff verification show test/lint/typecheck/test-suite commands only, with no interactiveChecks/manual watch CLI evidence.

summary: Review passed. Compared against the original failing commit (74e94f2c12a0a1467f952ffea828297837cd818f), this fix (e077fb11802101d2dc06fadb50a28e23f54966ef) adds startup pre-existing ID tracking and corresponding tests so pre-existing rows updated after watch start no longer emit spurious message_created/reply_created events, resolving the VAL-WATCH-003 blocking failure.
